title: 3 Days Gorilla Trekking Tour
description: An immersive 3-day adventure into Bwindi's ancient rainforest, home to half of the world's remaining mountain gorillas. Trek deep into the jungle to spend an unforgettable hour observing a mountain gorilla family in their natural habitat.

itinerary.title: Scenic Drive to Bwindi Impenetrable Forest
itinerary.desc: Our professional guide picks you up in Entebbe or Kampala. Embark on a highly scenic drive passing through the Equator, rolling hills, and local villages. Arrive at Bwindi Forest Resort for a welcome glass of local passionfruit juice and a gourmet dinner overlooking the misty forest canopy.

itinerary.title: The Life-Changing Gorilla Trekking Experience
itinerary.desc: Rise early for a rich organic breakfast. Gather at the park headquarters for a ranger briefing. Enter the ancient forest in search of your allocated gorilla family. Trekking takes between 2 to 6 hours depending on gorilla movement. Once found, enjoy a pristine, thrilling 1 hour standing mere meters away from these gentle giants. Return for a relaxing massage and evening campfire.

itinerary.title: Return Journey with Equator Stopover
itinerary.desc: Enjoy a leisurely morning watching Hornbills from your private terrace. Depart back to Kampala or Entebbe, stopping for a celebratory lunch at the Equator crossing. Transfer directly to Entebbe International Airport for your departure flight.

permitInfo: Uganda gorilla trekking permits must be secured well in advance. Our pricing fully includes the official $800 permit issued by the Uganda Wildlife Authority.
bestTime: June to September, and December to February (dry seasons make trekking trails less muddy and easier to navigate).
packingList: Long-sleeved shirt and trousers to protect from nettles
Camera with a fast zoom lens (No Flash allowed)

reviews.author: Emily Watson
reviews.text: Looking into the eyes of a silverback gorilla from 5 meters away was a spiritual experience. Horizon Chasers coordinated every detail flawlessly.

reviews.author: Jean-Pierre Laurent
reviews.text: Un service incroyable! Guide très professionnel et lodge luxueux en pleine jungle.

title: 5 Days Queen Elizabeth & Chimps
description: Track chimpanzees, view the legendary tree-climbing lions of Ishasha, and enjoy boat safaris alongside massive herds of elephants, hippos, and crocodiles in Uganda's most diverse national park.

itinerary.title: Journey to Queen Elizabeth via Crater Highlands
itinerary.desc: Depart Kampala, heading west through lush tea estates and the spectacular Rwenzori Mountains backdrop. Arrive at Mweya Safari Lodge for a twilight dinner overlooking the Kazinga Channel.

itinerary.title: Sunrise Game Drive & Kazinga Channel Boat Safari
itinerary.desc: Set out for an early game drive on the Kasenyi plains to view leopards, lions, Uganda kobs, and giant forest hogs. In the afternoon, embark on an exclusive boat cruise on Kazinga Channel to watch elephants bathing and thousands of hippos and birds.

itinerary.title: Search for Tree-Climbing Lions in Ishasha
itinerary.desc: Drive to the southern Ishasha sector. Search the giant sycamore fig trees for the rare, famous tree-climbing lions lounging in the canopy. Spend the night at a luxurious wilderness camp beside the Ishasha River.

itinerary.title: Chimpanzee Tracking in Kyambura Gorge
itinerary.desc: Descend into the dramatic 'Valley of Apes'—Kyambura Gorge. This subterranean rainforest is home to a habituated troop of chimps. Spend the morning observing their energetic social behavior. Afternoon crater lake photography drive.

itinerary.title: Scenic Transfer back via Royal Drum Makers
itinerary.desc: Return to Kampala, enjoying lunch at Mpambire village to watch traditional drum makers showcase centuries-old musical craftsmanship. Drop-off in Entebbe.

permitInfo: Kyambzee permits are included in this package. Places are limited, so early booking is highly recommended.
bestTime: January, February, June, July, and August.

reviews.author: Marcus & Sophia
reviews.text: Seeing three lionesses sleeping peacefully in a fig tree was incredible! The boat cruise was packed with elephants.

title: 7 Days Ultimate Luxury Fly-in Safari
highlights: Private boat to bottom of Murchison Falls
description: Fly directly into Uganda's most remote safari destinations. Avoid long driving hours and maximize your time in Kidepo Valley—often named Africa's finest wilderness—and the legendary Murchison Falls.

itinerary.title: Scenic Domestic Flight to Kidepo Valley
itinerary.desc: Board a luxury charter aircraft from Entebbe. Soar north over the Nile, landing directly in Kidepo Valley National Park. Check into Apoka Safari Lodge, built on a giant granite kopje with views of roaming herds of buffalo and zebras.

itinerary.title: Untamed Game Drives & Ik Cultural Visit
itinerary.desc: Set out into the Narus Valley. Kidepo boasts wildlife found nowhere else in Uganda, including cheetahs, ostriches, and bat-eared foxes. Afternoon hike to meet the remote mountain Ik community, learning about their ancient heritage.

itinerary.title: Flight to Murchison Falls National Park
itinerary.desc: Board your private charter flight to Murchison Falls. Touch down in the heart of the park and transfer to Baker's Lodge. Enjoy a private candlelit dinner on the banks of the mighty Nile River.

itinerary.title: Morning Game Drive & Launch Cruise to the Falls
itinerary.desc: Explore the northern delta for Rothschild giraffes, lions, and hyenas. After lunch, take an exclusive private boat safari up-river to the base of Murchison Falls, where the entire Nile squeezes through a 7-meter gorge with thunderous force.

itinerary.title: Hike to the Top of the Falls & Delta Cruise
itinerary.desc: Hike up to the dramatic Top of the Falls to feel the ground vibrate. Afternoon private delta boat trip to spot rare shoebill storks in the papyrus wetlands.

itinerary.title: Rhino Tracking at Ziwa Sanctuary
itinerary.desc: Morning drive south to Ziwa Rhino Sanctuary. Embark on an exciting foot patrol with rangers to track and photograph Uganda's only wild Southern White Rhinos up close.

itinerary.title: Flight back to Entebbe
itinerary.desc: A short final domestic flight back to Entebbe. Relax at a beach-side hotel lounge before your international departure.

permitInfo: Includes all local flight taxes, national park entry concessions, and specialized private ranger fees.
bestTime: Year-round, with dry months of December-February being exceptional for cheetah sightings in Kidepo.
packingList: Warm jacket for open air morning drives
Slip-on comfortable shoes for aircraft travel

reviews.author: William Sterling
reviews.text: A masterpiece of a holiday. Flying between Kidepo and Murchison saved so much time and the lodges are world-class. Absolute luxury in the wild.

title: 10 Days Birding & Cultural Odyssey
description: A specialized, peaceful tour designed for birding enthusiasts and cultural adventurers. Spot over 450 bird species and connect deeply with Uganda's ancient forest tribal kingdoms.

itinerary.title: Arrival & Birding in Entebbe Botanical Gardens
itinerary.desc: Welcome to Uganda. Spend the afternoon birdwatching along the shores of Lake Victoria in Entebbe, looking for Great Blue Turacos, hornbills, and weaver birds.

itinerary.title: Shoebill Search in Mabamba Swamp
itinerary.desc: Navigate the narrow papyrus channels of Mabamba Wetlands in a traditional wooden canoe to locate the prehistoric-looking Shoebill Stork. Transfer to Fort Portal.

itinerary.title: Semuliki Forest Birding & Hot Springs
itinerary.desc: Trek the Semuliki rainforest, representing the eastern limit of the great Congo Basin forest. Watch Sempaya Hot Springs boil water naturally. Search for Nkulengu Rail and Hornbills.

itinerary.title: Cultural Heritage of Tooro Kingdom
itinerary.desc: Visit the royal palace of Tooro Kingdom. Walk through Fort Portal's historical sites and enjoy lunch prepared by a local elder using organic volcanic soils garden crops.

itinerary.title: Birding in Kibale National Park Forest Canopy
itinerary.desc: Look for Green-breasted Pitta in the early hours. Walk the Bigodi Wetland Sanctuary boardwalk, a community-run sanctuary rich in primates and over 200 bird species.

itinerary.title: Scenic Transfer to Queen Elizabeth Park
itinerary.desc: Game drive en route. Birding along Kazinga Channel for water birds, fish eagles, and African skimmers.

itinerary.title: Batwa Cultural Experience
itinerary.desc: Walk into the forest with Batwa Pygmy guides. Experience how they hunted, collected wild honey, and used herbal medicine for thousands of years in Bwindi.

itinerary.title: Scenic Transfer to Lake Mburo National Park
itinerary.desc: Drive to Uganda's smallest savannah park. Evening boat cruise to spot African Finfoot and Giant Kingfishers.

itinerary.title: Walking Safari & Ankole Longhorn Culture
itinerary.desc: A rare guided nature walk amongst zebras and impalas. Afternoon visit to an Ankole cattle ranching community to learn about the cultural importance of the majestic long-horned cows.

itinerary.title: Equator Craft Shopping & Departure
itinerary.desc: Transfer back to Entebbe. Stop at royal drum makers and handcraft markets for souvenirs. Evening airport transfer.

permitInfo: Includes all custom forest and swamp ranger birding fees and community guide stipends.
bestTime: September to April when migratory birds from Europe are present in Uganda.
packingList: Field guide book to East African birds
High capacity SD cards for photography

reviews.author: Karen & David
reviews.text: We spotted the Shoebill within 30 minutes! Our guide, Joseph, knew every single bird call. An exceptional trip.
